verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane cargo receptor of the p24 family, predominantly resident in the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment and cis-Golgi where it cycles via COPI/COPII vesicles. However, TMED10 is documented to reach the plasma membrane as part of its secretory cycling itinerary, with a minority surface pool detectable by surface biotinylation and antibody labeling on intact cells. Its TM topology places the lumenal/GOLD domain on the extracellular face when at the PM. This is classic dual localization with a minor but real surface fraction.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether the PM minority pool is robust/reproducible enough to credit as dual_localization vs endomembrane_resident with only trafficking-transit exposure.
